dataset: Synthetic portfolio data (approximate early 2024 US stock prices)

pi_items.source: Cross-referenced setup_task.sh buyportfolio.csv heredoc (line 44) with live environment CSV dump. Units * price = 150 * 185.5 = 27825.0 matches CSV Purchase Value. Confirmed visually in JStock GUI screenshot (01_initial_screenshot.png).
Cross-referenced setup_task.sh buyportfolio.csv heredoc (line 45) with live environment CSV dump. 80 * 420 = 33600.0 matches. Confirmed visually in JStock GUI.
Cross-referenced setup_task.sh buyportfolio.csv heredoc (line 46) with live environment CSV dump. 35 * 615 = 21525.0 matches. Confirmed visually in JStock GUI.
Cross-referenced setup_task.sh buyportfolio.csv heredoc (line 47) with live environment CSV dump. 40 * 160 = 6400.0 matches. Confirmed visually in JStock GUI.

Cross-referenced with memo file (setup_task.sh line 100): 'Deposit $25,000 on March 1, 2024 / Comment: Q1 capital contribution'. The keyword 'capital' appears in the memo's prescribed comment. Verifier checks for 'capital' OR 'q1' OR 'contribution' in comment field (verifier.py line 100).

Cross-referenced with memo file (setup_task.sh line 102): 'Record Apple dividend of $111.00 received March 10, 2024 / Comment: Q1 2024 dividend'. The keyword 'dividend' appears in the memo's prescribed comment. Dividend dialog confirmed accessible in live env (07f_dividend_dialog_v2.png).

Cross-referenced with memo file (setup_task.sh line 106): 'Sell 50 shares of AAPL at $215.00/share' under March 15, 2024 header. Sell dialog confirmed accessible in live environment. Verifier checks sellportfolio.csv for AAPL entry (verifier.py line 131).

Cross-referenced with memo file (setup_task.sh line 107): 'Sell 15 shares of NVDA at $750.00/share' under March 15, 2024 header. Verifier checks sellportfolio.csv for NVDA entry (verifier.py line 138).

Independent computation: floor($25,000 / $105.00) = floor(238.0952...) = 238. Memo specifies '$25,000 into Exxon Mobil (XOM) at $105.00/share' with instruction to 'Buy the maximum whole shares (round DOWN)'. Computation confirmed via Python math.floor(25000/105.0) = 238.

Independent computation: floor($20,000 / $58.00) = floor(344.8276...) = 344. Memo specifies '$20,000 into Coca-Cola (KO) at $58.00/share'. Computation confirmed via Python math.floor(20000/58.0) = 344.

Independent computation: floor($12,000 / $162.50) = floor(73.8462...) = 73. Memo specifies '$12,000 into Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) at $162.50/share'. Computation confirmed via Python math.floor(12000/162.5) = 73. Note: JNJ already exists in portfolio at $160.00; the new buy at $162.50 creates a SECOND entry. The export_result.sh disambiguates by checking price proximity (abs(p - 162.50) < 2.0).

Independent computation: 185.50 * 0.95 = 176.225, rounded to nearest cent = $176.23 (conventional round-half-up) or $176.22 (banker's rounding). Metadata uses $176.23. Both are within the verifier tolerance of +/-$2.00. Rise above: 185.50 * 1.10 = 204.05 exactly. Memo says 'Fall Below alert at 5% below its purchase price, Rise Above at 10% above'. Purchase price from existing portfolio is $185.50.
Independent computation: 420.00 * 0.95 = 399.00 exactly. 420.00 * 1.10 = 462.00 exactly. Both are clean numbers.
Independent computation: 615.00 * 0.95 = 584.25 exactly. 615.00 * 1.10 = 676.50 exactly.

Independent computation: 160.00 * 0.95 = 152.00 exactly. 160.00 * 1.10 = 176.00 exactly. Note: JNJ has an existing position at $160.00 AND a new buy at $162.50. The memo says 'For stocks with multiple buy entries, use the earliest purchase price', so $160.00 is correct.
Independent computation: 105.00 * 0.95 = 99.75 exactly. 105.00 * 1.10 = 115.50 exactly. XOM is a new position; purchase price is from the memo ($105.00/share).
Independent computation: 58.00 * 0.95 = 55.10 exactly. 58.00 * 1.10 = 63.80 exactly. KO is a new position; purchase price is from the memo ($58.00/share).

Cross-referenced with memo file (line 116): 'Create watchlist "Q1 Rebalance Watch"'. Also confirmed in export_result.sh (line 7) which looks for this exact directory name. Verified in live environment that this watchlist does NOT pre-exist (02_watchlist_dirs.txt shows only 'My Watchlist').

Cross-referenced with memo file (line 125): 'Export updated buy portfolio to: /home/ga/Documents/portfolio_q1_export.csv'. Also confirmed in export_result.sh (line 8) and verifier.py. Live testing confirmed /home/ga/Documents/ is writable by user ga (09_write_permissions.txt).

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic/approximate prices loosely based on early 2024 US stock values
pi_items.source: Compared metadata prices against actual Yahoo Finance closing prices: AAPL Jan 2 2024 close was ~$185.64 (metadata $185.50 — close), MSFT was ~$374.51 (metadata $420.00 — significantly higher), NVDA was ~$560 (metadata $615.00 — significantly higher), JNJ was ~$155-160 (metadata $160.00 — close). Prices are synthetic/approximate, not actual closing prices.
O*NET code 11-3031.01 corresponds to 'Treasurers and Controllers', not 'Portfolio Manager'. This is a minor metadata field that does not affect task execution or verification.

privileged_info_summary: This is a quarterly portfolio rebalancing task in JStock. The starting portfolio contains AAPL (150 shares @ $185.50, Jan 2 2024), MSFT (80 shares @ $420.00, Jan 2 2024), NVDA (35 shares @ $615.00, Jan 15 2024), and JNJ (40 shares @ $160.00, Jan 15 2024), with a pre-existing $100,000 deposit. The agent must read a memo at /home/ga/Documents/q1_rebalance_memo.txt and execute 10 actions: (1) Record a $25,000 deposit on March 1, 2024 with comment containing 'capital'; (2) Record an AAPL dividend of $111.00 on March 10, 2024 with comment containing 'dividend'; (3) Sell 50 AAPL shares at $215.00 on March 15, 2024; (4) Sell 15 NVDA shares at $750.00 on March 15, 2024; (5) Buy 238 shares of XOM at $105.00 on March 15, 2024 (computed as floor($25,000/$105.00)); (6) Buy 344 shares of KO at $58.00 on March 15, 2024 (computed as floor($20,000/$58.00)); (7) Buy 73 shares of JNJ at $162.50 on March 15, 2024 (computed as floor($12,000/$162.50) — this is a SECOND JNJ entry, distinct from the existing 40 shares at $160.00); (8) Create a watchlist named exactly 'Q1 Rebalance Watch' containing all 6 stocks (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, JNJ, XOM, KO); (9) Set Fall Below and Rise Above alerts for each stock using 5% below and 10% above the earliest purchase price: AAPL ($176.23/$204.05), MSFT ($399.00/$462.00), NVDA ($584.25/$676.50), JNJ ($152.00/$176.00 — uses original $160 price, not the new $162.50), XOM ($99.75/$115.50), KO ($55.10/$63.80); (10) Export the buy portfolio to /home/ga/Documents/portfolio_q1_export.csv. The agent applies a $4.95 broker fee to each trade (but this is not scored by the verifier). All GUI operations (Buy, Sell, Cash/Deposit, Dividend dialogs, Watchlist creation via Watchlist > Multiple Watchlists menu, File > Save As for CSV export) have been confirmed accessible in the live environment.
